search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG98 persistent ear discharge otalgia abnormal tympanic membrane referral
candidate_sources_rejected: adult hearing guidance used to diagnose discharge cause
automatic culture, eardrop, antibiotic, imaging, procedure, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Otorrhoea may arise from canal, middle-ear, tympanic-membrane, postoperative, traumatic, or other causes.
No investigation, medicine, dose, treatment, procedure, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with persistent or recurrent ear discharge; children, tympanostomy tubes, acute infection, trauma, surgery, immunocompromise, and skull-base concern need separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary, audiology, urgent, or ENT follow-up.
UAE_applicability: NICE adult hearing guidance requires UAE microbiology, antimicrobial, audiology, imaging, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG98 exact signs, referral, and audiology sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG98 remains current and was reviewed in 2023.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult hearing guideline supports interval discharge, pain, hearing, tinnitus or vertigo context, relevant negatives, ENT examination, and clinician concern; it does not establish the cause of otorrhoea.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact audiology section supports functional impact, concerns, reviewed hearing tests, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending culture, drops, antibiotics, imaging, or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, laterality, duration, discharge character, pain, fever, hearing, vertigo, trauma, surgery, tubes, diabetes, examination, diagnosis, culture, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
